strategy: Waterworks finale team fight. Two alternating globals: light Doom and Gloom lets you cast Traps/Prisms (but strips positive charms), dark Doom and Gloom lets you cast Blades (but strips traps). They swap every ~4 rounds.
Match your setup to the global: lay Feints and traps during the light cycle, blades during the dark cycle. Protect one charm with Aegis and one trap with Indemnity across the swap.

When you attack, always activate a Trap (during light) or a Blade (during dark) — a single-target hit without one makes the enemy you hit cheat-cast (Sylster's 1,460-1,540 Ra, Scarecrow, or a Rebirth heal).

Multi-target traps (Windstorm, Mass Prisms) don't get stripped, and hits that also AoE (like Sun Serpent) still count — use these to play around the cycle. Never cast a Global, he replaces it with the current Doom and Gloom.
Funnel to one hitter, set up a trap-and-blade layered hit for a cycle where both are allowed, and burst. If Sylster dies the cheats pass to the Spellhammer Sorcerer, then the Water-Wing.
cheatNotes: During the light Doom and Gloom you may cast Traps and Prisms, but he Cleanse Charms three positive charms off the caster — save blades for the dark cycle.
Dissipate dispels this light cheat.
Negative charms (Weakness, Smoke Screen, Dispels) trigger the charm removal.
Pet spells don't trigger it.
Shadow Creature blades trigger it.
Aegis protects one charm from removal.
On the light cycle starting round 8 he strips three positive charms off all wizards and three traps off himself and his minions.
During the dark Doom and Gloom you may cast Blades and positive charms, but he Cleanse Wards one trap or prism off — save traps for the light cycle.
Vaporize dispels this dark cheat.
Elemental/Spirit Traps only lose their last part (Storm, Life).
Multi-target trap spells (Windstorm, Mass Prisms) don't get stripped — use them.
Indemnity protects one trap from removal.
On the first round of the dark cycle he strips three traps off himself and his minions.
Attack single-target without activating a trap (light) or blade (dark) and the enemy you hit cheat-casts a punish spell.
Sylster's punish is Ra for 1,460-1,540 Balance.
Unbalance dispels that Ra cheat.
The Spellhammer Sorcerer's punish is Scarecrow for 600 Death.
The Water-Wing's punish is a Rebirth healing 750 (375 under Doom and Gloom).
Enemy attack spells don't trigger the punish.
Single-target hits that also AoE (Sun Serpent) still count as answering the cycle.
Cast any Global and he replaces it with the current Doom and Gloom — don't bother.
Strangle can dispel either Doom and Gloom, but charms/traps still get removed if cast during the wrong global.
If Sylster dies, the Spellhammer Sorcerer takes over the cycle, restarting with the light Doom and Gloom.
If Sylster and the Spellhammer both die, the Water-Wing takes over the cycle.
